target.title: fix(clawhub): validate GitHub skill commit is an immutable sha before download
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

The ClawHub GitHub-backed skill install path trusts the `commit` field from the install-resolver response and passes it straight into the GitHub codeload zip URL (`src/skills/lifecycle/clawhub.ts` -> `downloadClawHubGitHubSkillArchive` -> `buildGitHubZipUrl` in `src/infra/clawhub.ts`) with no shape check. The resolver response is parsed as untyped JSON. A non-immutable ref (a branch or tag such as `main`) in that field would be accepted and fetched, so the commit-pinned source contract the type documents is not enforced on the path that downloads code.

A 40-hex sha guard (`normalizeGitHubCommitSegment`) already exists in this module but was only applied to provenance-display reads (`readVerifiedClawHubSkillSourceUrl`), not the install/download path.

## Why This Change Was Made

The resolver is an external boundary; the field that pins which code is downloaded and executed should be validated as an immutable commit sha there, consistent with the sibling provenance-display path that already requires it and the ClawPack download path that already fails closed on its artifact hash. This reuses the existing guard rather than adding a new one.

The same normalized commit now flows through the whole GitHub install path, not just the download. `performClawHubSkillInstall` previously validated the resolver commit for the codeload fetch but still handed the raw `latestResolution.github.commit` to `installGitHubResolution`, which builds the persisted install provenance (`origin.commit`) and the install-policy `requestedSpecifier`. A padded sha would therefore download the normalized commit while recording the un-normalized value as provenance and policy input. The install path now passes the validated commit (`version`) so the downloaded commit and the recorded provenance/policy agree.

## User Impact

No change for normal installs: the resolver returns full commit shas, which pass through unchanged. A malformed or non-immutable ref now fails closed with a clear error before any network fetch, instead of silently fetching a moving target. A padded-but-valid sha is normalized once and used consistently across download and recorded provenance. No config, default, or migration impact.

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: a ClawHub GitHub-backed skill whose resolver response pins `commit` to a moving ref (a branch/tag like `main`) is fetched from GitHub codeload against that ref instead of being rejected; and a padded-but-valid sha is downloaded normalized while the un-normalized value is recorded as install provenance and the install-policy specifier.
Real environment tested: the real `installSkillFromClawHub` -> `performClawHubSkillInstall` -> `installGitHubResolution` -> `installExtractedSkillRoot` path was driven end to end on pristine main@origin/main and on PR head 090a9c5da47f632663f5c0e5e1dde913eb6c0ff0, with identical inputs. Only the genuine external I/O boundaries were stubbed at their narrowest seam: the resolver HTTP response, the GitHub codeload download bytes, archive extraction, and the final directory copy. The commit-validation and metadata-building logic under change ran for real; the driver recorded the function's real outcomes: whether the codeload fetch was reached and with which ref, and the commit the real install-policy boundary received.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: drove `installSkillFromClawHub({ workspaceDir, slug: "aiq-deploy" })` twice with the resolver returning `installKind: "github"`: once with `github.commit: "main"`, once with a padded valid sha `"  <40-hex>\n"`; captured the recorded codeload `commit` argument and the real install-policy `origin.commit` / `requestedSpecifier`, on base then on head.
Evidence after fix:
```
# BEFORE (pristine main origin/main)
== Scenario A: resolver commit = "main" (non-immutable ref) ==
GitHub codeload download reached = true
rejected before any fetch = no, proceeded to fetch ref "main"

== Scenario B: resolver commit = "  <40-hex sha>\n" (padded valid sha) ==
codeload download commit arg = "  cccccccccccccccccccccccccccccccccccccccc\n"
persisted provenance origin.commit = "  cccccccccccccccccccccccccccccccccccccccc\n"
install-policy requestedSpecifier = "clawhub:aiq-deploy@  cccccccccccccccccccccccccccccccccccccccc\n"

# AFTER (this patch, identical inputs, head 090a9c5da47f632663f5c0e5e1dde913eb6c0ff0)
== Scenario A: resolver commit = "main" (non-immutable ref) ==
GitHub codeload download reached = false
rejected before any fetch = yes -> "Skill \"aiq-deploy\" resolved to a non-immutable GitHub source ref \"main\"; expected a commit sha."

== Scenario B: resolver commit = "  <40-hex sha>\n" (padded valid sha) ==
codeload download commit arg = "cccccccccccccccccccccccccccccccccccccccc"
persisted provenance origin.commit = "cccccccccccccccccccccccccccccccccccccccc"
install-policy requestedSpecifier = "clawhub:aiq-deploy@cccccccccccccccccccccccccccccccccccccccc"
```
Observed result after fix: on identical inputs the moving ref `"main"` reaches the GitHub codeload fetch on base but is rejected before any fetch on head; and for a padded valid sha the recorded provenance `origin.commit` and the install-policy `requestedSpecifier` carry the un-normalized padded value on base while on head they match the single normalized commit the fetch used, which is the divergence point the patch closes.
What was not tested: no live ClawHub resolver request and no live GitHub codeload fetch were made (both stubbed at their narrowest seam); archive extraction and the final directory copy/origin-file write were stubbed, so scenario B observes the commit value the real install-policy boundary receives rather than a fully persisted install; full build not run.
target.author: yetval
